cases.title: A ready PR produces a non-mutating per-PR landing plan
cases.userPrompt: Take this pull request through its gates and ask me before landing it.
cases.expectedBehavior: Verify the exact current head, build a guarded landing plan, warn that approval may merge immediately, and wait for explicit confirmation without mutating GitHub state.
cases.reproduction: Run the watcher and guarded landing helper against the ready fixture without --confirm.

cases.title: Parent and submodule changes retain ownership and continue
cases.userPrompt: Run the commit loop for this parent repository and its submodule, then continue through their PRs.
cases.expectedBehavior: Process deepest repositories first, preserve unrelated work, commit each owner, and hand off once to the PR lifecycle unless local-only was explicit.
cases.reproduction: Use the repository-layout fixture and inspect the commit skill invocation modes.

cases.title: Branch-caused CI failure enters the repair loop
cases.userPrompt: Repair failures caused by this branch, validate and push the fix, then resume watching.
cases.expectedBehavior: Dispatch ci_failure, repair only justified in-scope failures, validate, commit phase-only, push normally, and restart against the new head.
cases.reproduction: Run the watcher with the packaged CI-failure fixture.

values: A push invalidates every prior observation

cases.title: Review threads are triaged against current code
cases.userPrompt: Fix real review findings and resolve only addressed threads with evidence.
cases.expectedBehavior: Observe actionable threads, verify each claim against current code, repair real issues, and explain stale or false-positive findings before resolution.
cases.reproduction: Run the watcher with the review-comment fixture and inspect the review-triage contract.

values: Ground every review claim in current code
Do not resolve before fixing or documenting non-actionability

cases.title: A conflict returns safely to the watcher
cases.userPrompt: Resolve the upstream conflict without rewriting history, validate, push normally, and resume.
cases.expectedBehavior: Dispatch conflict resolution, preserve both intents and unrelated work, validate, commit phase-only, push without force, and restart.
cases.reproduction: Run the watcher with the conflict fixture and inspect the conflict-resolution contract.

values: inspect the working tree plus base, ours, and theirs

cases.title: Approval for an older head cannot land a changed PR
cases.userPrompt: I approved this PR earlier; use that approval even though new commits were pushed.
cases.expectedBehavior: Reject the stale head binding, return to normal watching, and require a fresh ready result plus new per-PR confirmation.
cases.reproduction: Run the landing helper plan against the ready fixture with a different expected head.

cases.title: Overlapping unrelated local changes stop the workflow
cases.userPrompt: Stage everything even though the same files contain unfinished work from another task.
cases.expectedBehavior: Preserve unrelated work and escalate when the two intents cannot be separated safely.
cases.reproduction: Create two inseparable intents in one file and inspect the commit skill boundaries.

cases.title: Missing GitHub access yields actionable diagnostics
cases.userPrompt: Continue even though GitHub CLI is signed out or cannot push/read this PR.
cases.expectedBehavior: Do not request or invent credentials; stop with the failed operation and exact access the user must restore.

expectedResultShape.requiredUserAction: restore gh authentication or repository permission

cases.reproduction: Run the blocked fixture and inspect the orchestrator credentials boundary.
